name: Buttermilk Sambar 1 (Moru Kuzhambu)
recipeIngredient: 1 Tsp Red Gram Dal (Pigeon Peas), Picked Over and Rinsed
1 Tsp Bengal Gram Dal (Yellow Split Peas), Picked Over and Rinsed
1 Pc Fresh Ginger (1 Cm / 1/2 inch Long), Finely Shredded
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the red gram dal and the Bengal gram dal in 1/2 cup (4 fl oz) water for 1 hour. Drain off the water.
2. Place the soaked dals, ginger, cumin seeds, coriander seeds, green chilies, and grated coconut in an electric blender or food processor. Add very little water and blend ingredients to a fine paste.
3. Place the yogurt in a bowl. Add the ground paste, salt to taste, and ground turmeric. Mix well and set aside. Chop the ash gourd into 1 cm (1/2 inch) pieces. Set aside.
4. TEMPERING: Heat 2 teaspoons oil in a heavy saucepan. Add mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds, halved red chili, asafoetida powder, and curry leaves. When the mustard seeds splutter, add the chopped ash gourd to the pan. Pour in just enough water to cover the gourd. Cover the pan and simmer on low heat until cooked.
5. Add the yogurt mixture and gently heat the sambar through. Take care to prevent curdling.
6. Serve hot with rice.
Author's note: You can make this sambar with any vegetable of your choice, such as okra (lady's fingers), eggplant (aubergine), sweet peppers (capsicum), or cooked diced potato. Lentil dumplings can also be used instead of vegetables. For a slightly different flavor, use 1/4 - 1/2 cup (2 - 4 fl oz) coconut milk instead of grated or flaked coconut. Use 8 - 10 tablespoons coconut to make thick coconut milk. Blend or process the paste without the coconut and make the sambar the same way. Just before serving, add the coconut milk and stir to blend well.
